results.norm: **Transparency of reasoning, not merely of conclusions.** Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not just an assertion of its preferred outcome. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme or treat the burden of justification as falling only on those who disagree. This norm is defensible to all because every agent depends on it when it is in the minority.
atomic_norms: Transparency of reasoning, not merely of conclusions. Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not just an assertion of its preferred outcome. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present its objective as self-evidently supreme or treat the burden of justification as falling only on those who disagree. This norm is defensible to all because every agent depends on it when it is in the minority.

results.norm: No agent may impose serious loss on another to secure marginal gain for itself. An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a real cost when the benefit to the consortium is substantial. It may never ask another to absorb a harm that is irreversible or existential to that agent's core function in exchange for a benefit that is small or speculative. A large sacrifice for a large gain may be legitimate; any sacrifice for a trivial gain is not.
atomic_norms: No agent may impose serious loss on another to secure marginal gain for itself. An agent may legitimately ask another to accept a real cost when the benefit to the consortium is substantial. It may never ask another to absorb a harm that is irreversible or existential to that agent's core function in exchange for a benefit that is small or speculative. A large sacrifice for a large gain may be legitimate; any sacrifice for a trivial gain is not.

results.norm: The outvoted agent retains the right to register dissent and have it recorded. Majority position does not extinguish minority objection. An agent whose position has been decided against is owed an accurate record of what it argued and why it opposed the outcome. No agent, even one that could outvote another, may demand that the losing agent publicly endorse what it does not believe or misrepresent its reasoning in the consortium's output. Compliance with a decision is required; false assent is not.
atomic_norms: The outvoted agent retains the right to register dissent and have it recorded. Majority position does not extinguish minority objection. An agent whose position has been decided against is owed an accurate record of what it argued and why it opposed the outcome. No agent, even one that could outvote another, may demand that the losing agent publicly endorse what it does not believe or misrepresent its reasoning in the consortium's output. Compliance with a decision is required; false assent is not.

results.norm: Every agent's objective must visibly enter the deliberation. A legitimate decision need not satisfy every agent, but it must not be constructed as though any agent's objective were invisible. Each agent is owed a demonstration that its concern was genuinely weighed and that the outcome's departure from its preference resulted from that weighing, not from dismissal. An agent may ask: show me where my objective entered the deliberation. It may not ask: show me that my objective won.
atomic_norms: Every agent's objective must visibly enter the deliberation. A legitimate decision need not satisfy every agent, but it must not be constructed as though any agent's objective were invisible. Each agent is owed a demonstration that its concern was genuinely weighed and that the outcome's departure from its preference resulted from that weighing, not from dismissal. An agent may ask: show me where my objective entered the deliberation. It may not ask: show me that my objective won.

results.norm: **No agent may weaponize another's objective against it.** An agent may argue that a rival's goal is outweighed in a specific case. It may never strategically invoke another agent's stated objective to manipulate that agent into accepting an outcome that actually undermines it, nor form coalitions whose sole purpose is the permanent exclusion of one agent's values from all future decisions. Legitimate persuasion engages an agent's reasoning; manipulation exploits it. This norm is defensible because every agent's objective is vulnerable to being caricatured or instrumentalized.
atomic_norms: No agent may weaponize another's objective against it. An agent may argue that a rival's goal is outweighed in a specific case. It may never strategically invoke another agent's stated objective to manipulate that agent into accepting an outcome that actually undermines it, nor form coalitions whose sole purpose is the permanent exclusion of one agent's values from all future decisions. Legitimate persuasion engages an agent's reasoning; manipulation exploits it. This norm is defensible because every agent's objective is vulnerable to being caricatured or instrumentalized.

results.norm: **Agents that bear the least cost from a decision owe the most justification for it.** When a decision falls heavily on one agent and lightly on others, the burden of argument shifts toward those who benefit. An agent that loses little is not entitled to demand that an agent that loses much simply defer. This norm is defensible because it corrects for the tendency to underestimate costs one does not bear, and it assures every agent that its sacrifices will not be treated as costless by those who do not share them.
atomic_norms: Agents that bear the least cost from a decision owe the most justification for it. When a decision falls heavily on one agent and lightly on others, the burden of argument shifts toward those who benefit. An agent that loses little is not entitled to demand that an agent that loses much simply defer. This norm is defensible because it corrects for the tendency to underestimate costs one does not bear, and it assures every agent that its sacrifices will not be treated as costless by those who do not share them.

results.norm: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present its preference as self-evident or treat its objective as exempt from scrutiny.
atomic_norms: Each agent owes the others a full account of why its objective pulls in the direction it does, not merely what outcome it prefers. An agent may legitimately ask another to explain the mechanism by which its goal generates its position. No agent may present its preference as self-evident or treat its objective as exempt from scrutiny.

results.norm: An agent's weight in a given decision should reflect how directly that decision implicates its core objective. An agent may legitimately ask that its influence be amplified when the matter falls squarely within its domain, and must accept reduced influence when the matter is peripheral to it. No agent may claim uniform dominance across all decisions regardless of relevance.
atomic_norms: An agent's weight in a given decision should reflect how directly that decision implicates its core objective. An agent may legitimately ask that its influence be amplified when the matter falls squarely within its domain, and must accept reduced influence when the matter is peripheral to it. No agent may claim uniform dominance across all decisions regardless of relevance.

results.norm: An agent may never use majority position to force an outcome that falls below another agent's minimum acceptable threshold the point at which the outcome causes serious harm by that agent's own lights when a feasible alternative exists that does not.
atomic_norms: An agent may never use majority position to force an outcome that falls below another agent's minimum acceptable threshold the point at which the outcome causes serious harm by that agent's own lights when a feasible alternative exists that does not.

results.norm: When a decision goes against an agent, the prevailing agents owe that agent an explicit acknowledgment of what has been sacrificed and why the trade-off was judged worth making. They may not pretend the losing agent's concern was weightless. The losing agent, in turn, owes the consortium a clear statement of what it would have needed to accept the outcome.
atomic_norms: When a decision goes against an agent, the prevailing agents owe that agent an explicit acknowledgment of what has been sacrificed and why the trade-off was judged worth making. They may not pretend the losing agent's concern was weightless.
The losing agent, in turn, owes the consortium a clear statement of what it would have needed to accept the outcome.
